prompts: Projectile motion follows a parabolic trajectory due to the influence of gravity acting on the object. As a projectile is launched, it possesses an initial velocity with both horizontal and vertical components. The horizontal motion is constant, while the vertical motion is influenced by gravitational acceleration, causing the object to rise until reaching a peak, then descend back to the ground. The path traced by the projectile can be described as an arc, forming a parabola when air resistance is negligible, illustrating the relationship between initial velocity, angle of launch, gravity, and the eventual range and height achieved.
